mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales indicates strengthening household demand and near-term revenue expectations; higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports RSAFS value of 366404 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk; when UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 returns turn negative over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports UNRATE value of 9.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered 60-day returns are not below unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Very low policy rates combined with stable consumer prices reduce discount-rate pressure without creating inflation tightening risk; FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and non-accelerating CPI predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports FEDFUNDS value of 0.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate stable-CPI trigger has a 20-day hit rate below 50% or if its mean return is not higher than low FEDFUNDS alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent contraction in industrial production indicates weakening goods-sector output and earnings revisions; three consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports INDPRO value of 102.2666 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO contraction signals are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside skew is not larger than unconditional downside skew.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts recovery leads construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit creation; when HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average, S&P 500 returns are positive over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports HOUST value of 626.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if upward HOUST moving-average crossings do not deliver higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-crossing months or if hit rate is below 52% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation during a policy-rate uptrend tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations; accelerating CPIAUCSL with rising FEDFUNDS predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth > 3% and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-and-rate trigger has a positive 20-day return hit rate above 55% or if its drawdown frequency is not higher than periods with only rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion accompanied by rising retail sales confirms that job growth is translating into spending; positive PAYEMS momentum together with positive RSAFS momentum predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports PAYEMS value of 130161.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS and RSAFS momentum signal fails to outperform either single-series signal over 5 trading days or if out-of-sample Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth with falling unemployment identifies a self-sustaining recovery in aggregate demand; positive GDPC1 growth and declining UNRATE predict positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 and UNRATE declines over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot reports GDPC1 value of 13254.7 as of t-4mo
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with improving labor market
failure_condition: Reject if triggered recovery observations have lower 60-day average S&P 500 returns than unconditional observations or if positive-return hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.
